info.title: Code Guard — security scan for AI-generated code
info.description: 53% of code is now AI-written and ~25% of it is vulnerable. Code Guard is the in-loop check a coding agent runs on its OWN generated code/diff before committing. Deterministic rule engine (no LLM) for the high-frequency classes: command/code/SQL injection, SSRF, hardcoded secrets, weak crypto, unsafe deserialization, disabled TLS verification, XSS/template-injection. Returns findings (rule, severity, line, fix) + a verdict (pass/review/block). Fast first-line scanner, not a full audit replacement.

post.summary: Scan a code snippet for security vulnerabilities before committing/running it

example.code:
import os
os.system('echo ' + user_input)
obj = pickle.loads(data)

200.description: verdict (pass/review/block) + findings [{rule id, category, severity, line, code, message, remediation}] + counts.

post.summary: Scan only the ADDED lines of a unified diff (the agent's just-written change)

200.description: verdict + findings on the added lines (with correct new-file line numbers).

get.summary: The deterministic rule catalog Code Guard checks (coverage transparency)
